reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 triggered: Trust Boundary. Phase 03 correctly identified that config/params/loader.go::UnmarshalConfig (L29-73) loads fork epochs from YAML without validating strict ordering (Genesis < Altair < ... < Fulu). The merge() function at config/params/config.go:502 silently sorts entries by epoch, which could cause version-to-epoch decoupling if epochs are out of order in the YAML. However, the attack path requires the node operator to provide a misconfigured YAML file via the --chain-config-file CLI flag (validator/node/node.go:95-100). BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json explicitly categorizes local_validator configuration as SEMI_TRUSTED with in_scope_for_bug_bounty: false and notes 'Configuration issues requiring operator error are typically out-of-scope.' This is an operator misconfiguration issue, not an exploitable vulnerability via untrusted entry points.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Fork epoch constants must be strictly ordered: GENESIS < ALTAIR < ... < ELECTRA < FULU. (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, PROP-6a4369e9-inv-014)
